Fines ‘Any citizen may assess fines ayainst any lower clearance citizen. The charges and amount of the fine are immediately reported to The Computer, which must approve them before they become official, The mast com- mon fine is for damage to Computer proper- 'y, and is generally assessed by the citizen who originally assigned the property. Note: Assess. ing excessive fines isa treasonous offense. Un fortunately there are no published guidelines for appropriate fines. Here are a few examples of typical fines: ‘dirty jumpsuit — 3 credits * failing o protect a higher clearance citizen from discomfort — 10 credits functioning. laser pistol (obviously careless maintenance) — 200 credits careless use of incendiaries — 400 credits * careless incineration of an Infrared citizen — 200 credits 
I Serious Offenses 
A serious offense is any deliberate and premeditated act that harms The Computer, Alpha Complex, or its loyal citizens. Sure evidence of premeditation is a pattern of repeated minor offenses; ather evidence may come from eyewitness accounts, file eviews, 
character witnesses, and so on. Whenever a citizen is suspected of treason (disloyalty t0 The Computer, Commie sympathies, mutant powers, membership in a secret society, etc), but the evidence is insufficient to warrant ter- mination, the citizen may be convicted of the serious offense of Suspected Treason. The accused always has an opportunity to defend himself in. a hearing before his superiors and The Computer (which reviews all chargés of serious offenses).
